meta.title: Emotional Availability Test
meta.description: A private 500-statement reflection across 20 dimensions of emotional availability.

levels.label: Strong Emotional Availability
levels.desc: Your responses suggest consistent emotional awareness, openness, responsiveness, and capacity for connected boundaries.

levels.label: Generally Available
levels.desc: You appear emotionally available in many situations, with a few contexts where stress, vulnerability, or conflict may reduce access.

levels.label: Developing Availability
levels.desc: You show meaningful relational strengths alongside areas where expression, listening, trust, or repair could grow.

levels.label: Availability Under Strain
levels.desc: Emotional connection may feel difficult in several situations. Focused communication and relationship skills practice may help.

levels.label: Limited Current Availability
levels.desc: You may currently find emotional openness or responsiveness difficult. Consider gentle skills practice or support from a qualified counsellor if this causes distress.

questions.text: In my closest relationships, I can usually identify what I am feeling.
During an ordinary conversation, I often feel something strongly without knowing what it is.
When someone shares difficult news, I notice emotional changes before they strongly affect my behaviour.
After a misunderstanding, I recognise my feelings only after they have already shaped my actions.
When emotions become intense, I can usually identify what I am feeling.
While spending time together, I often feel something strongly without knowing what it is.
